message:
fix: harden cryptographic comparisons, fix config merge order, and correct behavioral bugs

Security:
- Replace naive SHA256(key||payload) signing with HMAC-SHA256 in
  root_pointer.rs to prevent length-extension attacks
- Add constant_time_eq() for MAC/signature verification in root_pointer.rs,
  remote_cap.rs, and trust_card.rs to prevent timing side-channel attacks

Bug fixes:
- config.rs: apply base overrides before profile overrides so
  profile-specific values take precedence (was reversed); un-nest
  let-chains for stable Rust compatibility
- intent_firewall.rs: fix priority semantics so higher-numbered rules win;
  reverse iteration order in match_rule()
- sybil_defense.rs: replace naive min/max burst detection with sliding
  window algorithm; increase default trim_ratio from 0.1 to 0.2
- bpet_migration_gate.rs: handle unknown rollout phases gracefully instead
  of panicking via expect()
- n_version_oracle.rs: include risk-tier-specific reasons in divergence
  classification messages instead of generic "digest mismatch"
- vef/control_integration.rs: check evidence hash emptiness on all states,
  not only Verified
- replay_bundle.rs: remove secondary sort tiebreakers (event_type,
  payload_hash) that masked original insertion order
- verifier_economy/mod.rs: fix inverted check_selective_reporting test
  assertions
- trust_card.rs: restructure cache lookup as early-return pattern for
  efficiency and correctness; verify signature on cached card before use
- supply_chain/manifest.rs: project publisher_signature, trust_chain_ref,
  min_engine_version, and content_hash into engine manifest

Code quality:
- Break long single-line method chains into multi-line formatting across
  20+ files for line-length compliance (no behavioral changes)
- Use is_some_and() and let-chains where idiomatic (dpor_exploration.rs,
  lane_scheduler.rs, cx_first_policy.rs)
- Regenerate verification artifacts with current date
- Add bd-17ds test coverage epic and 24 sub-beads to issues.jsonl

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -283,17 +283,30 @@ pub fn run_harness(
                 let _ = event_codes::ORACLE_DIVERGENCE_CLASSIFIED;
                 let _ = event_codes::ORACLE_RISK_TIER_ASSIGNED;
 
+                let reason = match risk_tier {
+                    RiskTier::High => format!(
+                        "High: divergence at {} against {} ({} reference runtime(s) configured)",
+                        sample.boundary_name,
+                        rt.runtime_id,
+                        config.reference_runtimes.len()
+                    ),
+                    RiskTier::Medium => format!(
+                        "Medium: single-reference divergence at {} against {}",
+                        sample.boundary_name, rt.runtime_id
+                    ),
+                    RiskTier::Low => format!(
+                        "Low: divergence below threshold at {} against {}",
+                        sample.boundary_name, rt.runtime_id
+                    ),
+                };
                 divergences.push(BoundaryDivergence {
                     divergence_id: format!("div-{div_counter:04}"),
                     boundary_name: sample.boundary_name.clone(),
                     franken_engine_output_digest: fe_digest.clone(),
                     reference_output_digest: ref_digest,
                     reference_runtime: rt.clone(),
                     risk_tier,
-                    classification_reason: format!(
-                        "digest mismatch at {} against {}",
-                        sample.boundary_name, rt.runtime_id
-                    ),
+                    classification_reason: reason,
                     l1_oracle_link: None,
                 });
             }
